mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the federal funds rate over the trailing 3 months raises the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and tightens financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as valuations compress.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.70% in May 2018 vintage, rising steadily since late 2015
failure_condition: Rank IC between 3-month FEDFUNDS change and forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or periods of rising FEDFUNDS show no significant return difference from periods of falling FEDFUNDS (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE falls below a historically low threshold indicating a fully tight labor market, wage pressure and late-cycle risk rise, prompting investors to discount future earnings more heavily and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.8% in May 2018, near multi-decade lows
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: When UNRATE < 4.0% is triggered, forward 60d SPX returns are positive in more than 60% of instances, or mean forward return in triggered periods is not significantly lower than the unconditional mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising FEDFUNDS and historically low UNRATE signals a Phillips-curve-driven tightening cycle where the Fed is compelled to accelerate rate hikes to contain wage inflation, and this combined signal predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days more strongly than either series alone.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=1.7% and UNRATE=3.8% co-occurring in May 2018
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with tight labor market
failure_condition: The joint-signal model's out-of-sample rank IC does not exceed the maximum of the two single-series models' rank IC by at least 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong month-over-month growth in RSAFS during a late-cycle expansion reflects resilient consumer spending that supports near-term corporate revenue beats, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days, but this effect weakens or reverses once the expansion matures past peak consumer confidence.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS vintage_value=501971 million in April 2018, indicating continued retail growth
failure_condition: In late-cycle expansion periods, RSAFS growth above threshold fails to produce positive 5d forward returns in over 55% of triggered instances, or the effect reverses sign in the final 12 months before an NBER-dated recession.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive month-over-month growth in INDPRO reflects expanding industrial output and corporate production capacity, which supports forward earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage_value=107.30 in May 2018 vintage showing continued industrial expansion
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing 3-month INDPRO growth and forward 20d returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or negative INDPRO growth periods do not show significantly lower returns than positive growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation crosses above a threshold consistent with the Fed's tolerance ceiling, markets price in accelerated future rate hikes, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL revised_value=250.792 as of May 2018 vintage, showing inflation trending above the Fed's 2% target
failure_condition: When CPI YoY exceeds 2.5%, forward 60d SPX returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the mean triggered return is not significantly lower than unconditional mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in HOUST during a late-cycle expansion signals an early rollover in housing demand, which historically leads broader economic slowdowns and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as investors anticipate weaker construction and durable goods spending.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST revised_value=1276 thousand units in April 2018, plateauing after prior expansion
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with housing market peak
failure_condition: During late-cycle regimes, 3-month HOUST declines fail to precede negative 60d forward returns in over 55% of instances, or rank IC stays below 0.03 across any 3-year rolling window restricted to late-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of decelerating INDPRO growth alongside decelerating RSAFS growth signals a simultaneous supply and demand slowdown that is not visible from either series alone, and this combined weakening predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as short-term momentum traders react to concurrent soft data.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth < 0 AND RSAFS month-over-month growth < 0
evidence_refs: fact:both INDPRO and RSAFS vintages as of mid-2018 showing positive but decelerating growth trajectories
regime_hint: any regime with concurrent supply and demand deceleration
failure_condition: The joint-trigger signal's forward 5d return distribution is not significantly more negative than either single-series decelerating-growth signal, tested via bootstrap comparison of mean returns over any 2-year window.
